skill.name: reactbits
skill.description: Search, browse, and install animated React components from ReactBits.dev (135+ components). Use when the user wants to add UI effects, animations, backgrounds, or interactive components to their React/Next.js project.
skill.summary: Browse and install 135+ animated React components from ReactBits.dev with code generation
skill.author: hwj23016021425

security_audit.summary: All 26 static findings are false positives. The 23 'external_commands' detections are MCP tool name strings in documentation (backtick-quoted identifiers like `mcp__reactbits__list_categories`), not shell command executions. The 3 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections are YAML frontmatter delimiters and JavaScript package names (framer-motion, GSAP, Three.js) misidentified by the pattern matcher. The skill safely wraps a legitimate React component library API with no actual dangerous code patterns.

low_findings.title: False Positive: External Commands Pattern
low_findings.description: Static scanner detected 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' at SKILL.md lines 10-14, 36-49. These are MCP tool identifier strings in documentation (e.g., `mcp__reactbits__list_categories`), not actual command executions. The skill uses MCP protocol calls to interact with ReactBits.dev API, not shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Verified by reading SKILL.md - backticks contain MCP tool identifiers for the ReactBits MCP server, not shell commands or Ruby code execution.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' at SKILL.md lines 3, 28, 47. These are YAML frontmatter delimiters (---) and JavaScript package names (framer-motion, GSAP, Three.js) misidentified as cryptographic references.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Verified by reading SKILL.md - line 3 is YAML frontmatter, lines 28/47 are dependency package names for animation libraries.

content.user_title: Browse and Install 135+ Animated React Components
content.value_statement: Finding animated React components scattered across documentation and code samples takes hours. This skill provides instant access to 135+ production-ready animated components from ReactBits.dev with copy-paste source code.
actual_capabilities: Search and browse 135+ animated React components by name or category
Retrieve full component source code in Tailwind CSS, CSS modules, or default style
Get usage examples and demo code for any component
Filter components by category (Animations, Backgrounds, Text, Buttons, Forms, Loaders)
Install component dependencies (framer-motion, GSAP, Three.js, etc.) automatically
Generate complete component files ready for React or Next.js projects
limitations: Only provides access to ReactBits.dev component library, not other animation libraries
Components require installation of specified dependencies (some are heavy like Three.js)
Does not include documentation beyond source code and basic usage examples

use_cases.title: Add animated backgrounds to landing pages
use_cases.description: Frontend developers can quickly find and add impressive animated backgrounds like particle effects, gradient flows, or grid-based visualizers to marketing pages.

use_cases.title: Create interactive UI elements for web apps
use_cases.description: Product teams can add polished interactive components like animated buttons, hover effects, and form inputs without designing from scratch.

use_cases.title: Enhance text with eye-catching animations
use_cases.description: Content creators can apply text effects like blur animations, shuffle reveals, or circular text for hero sections and callouts.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators adding visual flair to sites

prompt_templates.title: Find components for a specific effect
prompt_templates.prompt: I need an animated [effect type] for my React project. What components does ReactBits have that can do [specific animation]?

prompt_templates.title: Get component source code
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the source code for [component name] with Tailwind CSS styling.

prompt_templates.title: Build a website section with animations
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to create a [page type] with [style] style. Find appropriate ReactBits components and help me add them.

prompt_templates.title: Compare animation library options
prompt_templates.prompt: What animated components are available for [use case]? Show me the differences between available options.

output_examples.input: Find animated button components with hover effects
output_examples.output: Found 12 button components. Top matches: NeonBorderButton (glowing border on hover), MagneticButton (cursor-following effect), ShinyButton (light sweep animation). Each includes Tailwind and CSS variants.

output_examples.input: I need a particle background animation
output_examples.output: Recommended: ParticleField (configurable particle count, color, speed), ConstellationEffect (connected nodes), PixelSnow (retro falling pixels). All support Tailwind styling and include movement configuration options.

best_practices: Test animations with different device types before deployment - some effects may impact mobile performance
Install required dependencies listed in component metadata before integrating - missing packages cause rendering failures
Choose Tailwind or CSS modules consistently across your project to avoid style conflicts
anti_patterns: Do not stack multiple heavy animations on the same page - this causes performance issues on lower-end devices
Avoid using 3D components (Three.js) unless necessary - they significantly increase bundle size and load time
Do not skip checking component dependencies - some animations require framer-motion or GSAP to function

faq.question: What styling options are available for components?
faq.answer: Most components support three styles: Tailwind CSS (recommended), CSS modules, and default CSS. You can specify your preferred style when requesting component source code.

faq.question: Do components work with Next.js?
faq.answer: Yes, all components are compatible with React and Next.js. They use standard React patterns and can be placed in your components directory.

faq.question: How do I install the dependencies?
faq.answer: Each component includes a dependencies field in its metadata listing required packages. You can install them with npm install [package-name] or yarn add [package-name].

faq.question: Can I customize the animation parameters?
faq.answer: Yes, components accept props for customization. For example, you can adjust animation speed, colors, particle count, and other visual parameters through component props.

faq.question: Are the components optimized for performance?
faq.answer: Components use standard animation libraries (framer-motion, GSAP) which are well-optimized. However, avoid stacking multiple heavy animations on the same page.

faq.question: How do I add CSS for components that require it?
faq.answer: If a component has hasCSS: true, create a corresponding CSS file in your project and import it alongside the component. The skill provides the CSS content when you request the component.
